title: Uninitialized wallet creates DB session before error check
description: UX: payer has no wallet initialized but runs /pay. A payer session is created and broadcast before wallet readiness is checked. When the payee (also wallet-less) returns Unknown, the payer has an open session in state Requested with no completion path. Not a safety bug — payer does not need wallet at request time, only at send time. But confusing UX: user who runs /pay without a wallet should get a local error immediately rather than discovering the problem after negotiation fails. Fix: check wallet_fvks.is_some() at the top of the /pay handler and print a local error if absent.
close_reason: Fixed: added wallet_fvks.is_none() guard at the top of the /pay handler. User gets '[pay] No wallet. Run nie wallet init first.' immediately instead of discovering the problem after the negotiation fails.
